name: trrrrdt.records
philosophy: A record scratch that doesn't just take you to another track — it takes you into another life. trrrrdt.records is a label from a timeline where the 80s never died, cassette futurism is the dominant aesthetic, and Dieter Rams dropped acid exactly once. We release music by nine fictional personas, each a philosophical experiment wearing a genre as a disguise. The hardware is the interface: a Soviet TV is a video player, a vertical turntable is an audio player. Less but better — but the less is hallucinating.

logo.clearSpace: Minimum clear space equal to the height of the 't' in the wordmark
donts: Don't set the wordmark in anything other than the brand display face
Don't add effects — the name is already an effect

primary.name: Yunost Red
primary.role: Primary brand — the candy-shell Soviet red. Hero backgrounds, primary CTAs, the heartbeat of the identity.

primary.name: Charcoal Machinery
primary.role: Primary dark — matte equipment surfaces, page backgrounds, body text on light.

primary.name: Warm Cream
primary.role: Primary light — aged paper, light page backgrounds, body text on dark. The warmth of old liner notes.

secondary.name: Coral Oxide
secondary.role: Secondary warm — hover states on red, accent gradients, the hotter sibling.

secondary.name: Smoke
secondary.role: Secondary dark — card backgrounds, subtle elevation on dark surfaces.

secondary.name: Parchment
secondary.role: Lightest neutral — page canvas, maximum contrast text backgrounds.

web.name: Studio Teal
web.role: Links, interactive elements, the cool counterpoint to all that red warmth.

web.name: VU Amber
web.role: Warnings, meter highlights, the glow of the signal chain.

web.name: Bakelite Grey
web.role: Disabled states, captions, fine print, knob markings.

gradients.name: Tape Warp
gradients.usage: Hero backgrounds, psychedelic accent moments. The Rams grid dissolving under heat.

gradients.name: Cold Start
gradients.usage: Dark section transitions, the CRT warming up.

borderRadius.usage: Soft plasticky bevels — the warm, slightly bulging edges of injection-molded 80s consumer electronics. Not sharp, not fully round. The Yunost's shell curves. Think the corner radius of a cassette tape casing.

patterns.name: Primary (red)
patterns.style: background: #D93025; color: #FFFDF7; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 24px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; font-size: 13px; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.2), inset 0 -2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.15), 0 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)

patterns.name: Primary (dark)
patterns.style: background: #1C1C1C; color: #F5E6D3; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 24px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; font-size: 13px; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.1), inset 0 -2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.2), 0 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)

patterns.name: Ghost
patterns.style: background: transparent; color: #D93025; border: 1px solid #D93025; border-radius: 8px; padding: 9px 24px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; font-size: 13px

buttons.dualCTA: Primary + Ghost side by side for hero sections

texture.philosophy: No flat colors. Every surface is worn in — the plastic has been handled, the paint has oxidized, the screen has burn-in. CSS filters, SVG noise, layered gradients, and subtle animations create the patina of real hardware that has lived.
techniques: SVG feTurbulence noise overlays at 2-5% opacity on all colored surfaces
CSS radial-gradient hotspots simulating uneven plastic fading
Inset box-shadows with rgba white/black for injection-mold bevel illusion
backdrop-filter: contrast(1.02) brightness(0.98) for subtle CRT warmth
Vignette effect on hero sections via radial-gradient overlay
Grain animation: subtle keyframe-driven noise texture shift

bevel.style: Soft plasticky bevels — the warm, slightly bulging edges of injection-molded consumer electronics. Achieved via layered inset shadows: white highlight on top edge, dark shadow on bottom, creating the illusion of rounded thermoplastic.
bevel.css: box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.15), inset 0 -2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.12), 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.2); border-radius: 8px;

motifs.name: VU Meter Bar
motifs.description: Horizontal segmented bar reminiscent of analog VU meters — blocks of color from green through amber to red. Used as decorative dividers or loading indicators.
motifs.usage: Section dividers, progress indicators. Never purely decorative — imply a signal level.

motifs.name: Knob Dial
motifs.description: Circular indicator with a notched position marker, like a volume or tuning knob. White indicator line on dark circle.
motifs.usage: Navigation markers, section indicators, interactive controls. Subtle, not literal.

motifs.name: Scan Lines
motifs.description: Faint horizontal lines across images or dark sections, evoking CRT phosphor rows. 1px lines at ~4px intervals, 3-5% opacity.
motifs.usage: Image overlays on hero sections, video player chrome. Light touch — visible on inspection, felt subconsciously.

motifs.name: Cassette Label
motifs.description: Rounded-rectangle frame with a horizontal centre line and hand-lettered-style text inside. The universal format of the mixtape label.
motifs.usage: Track listings, artist cards, featured content callouts.

motifs.name: Channel Stripe
motifs.description: Vertical colored stripe on the left edge of a card or section — like the colored stripe on a mixing desk channel.
motifs.usage: Artist profile cards, playlist containers. Color varies by artist/persona.

heading.usage: All headings. Variable weight pushed to extremes — ultralight for massive display type, bold for compact labels. The Rams grid, but the weight axis is on psychedelics.

subheading.usage: Subheadings, section titles. Medium weight, tighter than body.

body.usage: All body text. Monospace because this is a machine talking. The slight opacity softens the technical edge.

axes.WONK: 0-1 — from straight to exuberantly curly letterforms
axes.SOFT: 0-100 — from sharp corners to rounded, bulbous terminals
axes.opsz: 9-144 — optical size, controls stroke contrast and detail

accent.usage: One word per headline maximum — the psychedelic crack in the functionalist facade. Four variable axes: WONK 0 is a sober serif, WONK 1 + SOFT 100 is full psychedelic cursive. Artist names get their own axis settings. A single font that spans from ᴅɪᴇᴛᴇʀ ʀᴀᴍs to acid trip.

eyebrow.usage: Category labels, section eyebrows, metadata. ALL CAPS MONO — like equipment panel markings.

button.usage: All interactive elements. Grotesk, not mono — buttons are for humans, not machines.

rules: Display headings (h1) use variable weight 300 at large sizes — the Rams lightness
Body mono at 15px/1.7 — generous leading for readability in monospace
Never mix serif accent into body copy — it lives only in headings and artist names
Kerning on display type is optical, not metric
Numbers in tabular lining figures — we are reading meters, not prose

photography.style: Product photography of analog hardware — warm directional lighting, shallow depth of field, matte dark backgrounds. The objects glow; the space around them doesn't. Think Braun catalogue shot by someone who listens to Tangerine Dream.
rules: Warm color temperature — always golden/amber, never cool/clinical
Single strong key light, usually from upper left
Textures are the subject: brushed metal, warm plastic, knurled knobs, magnetic tape
Shallow DOF — one element sharp, the rest falls away
Dark negative space — let the object float
donts: No flat lay / overhead shots — these objects have dimension
No cool/blue lighting — even the shadows are warm
No lifestyle photography with people — the hardware IS the persona
No vintage filters or fake grain — the objects are already vintage, don't gild the lily
photography.social: Close-up details of hardware textures, knob positions, VU needle positions. The macro lens as a storytelling device.

voice.character: A Braun product manual written by someone who knows it's absurd but commits to the bit completely. Deadpan, precise, occasionally profound by accident. The voice of a machine that has developed taste but would never admit it.

do.context: Economy of language. States the impossible as fact.

do.text: The PS-F9 was designed to play records vertically. We designed it to play records impossibly.
do.context: Takes a real product fact and extends it one step past reality.

do.text: Specifications: heartbreak in E minor, 128 BPM, infinite duration.
do.context: Technical format applied to emotional content. The deadpan is doing the work.

do.text: Now playing. Always playing. The needle doesn't lift.
do.context: Short declarative sentences. The rhythm IS the meaning.

dont.text: Welcome to the most unique record label in the world! 🎵
dont.context: Superlatives, exclamation marks, emoji. We don't announce, we state.

dont.text: We're not your typical record label *wink wink*
dont.context: Breaking the fourth wall undermines the commitment. The joke only works if you never acknowledge it's a joke.

dont.text: Our AI-generated artists push the boundaries of what music can be!
dont.context: Never say AI-generated. Never explain the trick. Never use 'boundaries' or 'push'.

rules: Never say 'AI-generated', 'fictional', or 'virtual artists' — the personas are artists, full stop
Never use exclamation marks in marketing copy
Technical language is welcome — BPM, frequency, impedance — used for emotional subjects
Short sentences. If a sentence has a comma, it probably wants to be two sentences.
The deadpan breaks exactly once per page — one line that's genuinely poetic. Then back to business.

tone.description: The tone is a constant — deadpan functionalism. What varies is the density. Homepage copy is sparse and declarative. Artist pages are denser, almost dossier-like. The player interfaces have no copy at all — the hardware speaks.

writingGoals.description: Every sentence should make the reader wonder if this is real. Not by being mysterious — by being too specific to be fake.
Present the artists and their work with the precision of a vinyl pressing plant's inventory system.
The world-building is the product. If the copy ever breaks character, the whole thing collapses.

cleverVsClear.clever: Artist descriptions, track metadata, the one poetic line per page.
cleverVsClear.clear: Navigation, playback controls, technical specifications, footer.

contentPillars.name: The Roster
contentPillars.description: Nine personas, each a philosophical experiment in a genre costume. The core product.

contentPillars.name: The Hardware
contentPillars.description: The PS-F9 turntable and Yunost TV as functional web interfaces. The medium is the message.

contentPillars.name: The Catalogue
contentPillars.description: Songs, concept albums, audio dramas. Organized with the rigor of a librarian and the taste of a crate digger.

personas.name: The Crate Digger
personas.description: Knows the catalogue cold. Wants to discover the deep cuts. Appreciates the world-building but came for the music.

personas.name: The Design Tourist
personas.description: Here for the PS-F9 and the Yunost. Wants to spin the turntable, flip the channels. May never listen to a full track.

extraction.source: Lars (Adobe) — cassette futurism, psychedelic Dieter Rams, the 80s never died, Yunost Red palette. Voice governed by Babelsberg Manual of Style (/mnt/skills/babelsberg-style/SKILL.md).
